Tech leads ship code.
Stop manually translating meetings into tasks.

Every sprint planning, code review, and design session ends the same way: someone has to translate the discussion into tickets. contextprompt sends an AI bot to your meetings, scans your repos, and extracts structured coding tasks with real file paths automatically.

Get started free

Free plan includes 1 hour/month. No credit card required.

The tech lead tax on every meeting

You're the bridge between discussion and execution. That costs more time than it should.

//

Post-meeting ticket writing

After every planning meeting, code review, or architecture discussion, someone — usually you — has to turn the conversation into actionable tickets. Generic meeting tools give you a wall of transcript text to work from, not structured tasks.

?!

Context dies between meetings

A developer picks up a ticket two days after the planning meeting. The file path wasn't captured. The "auth refactor" could mean three different things. They come back to you for clarification — pulling you out of flow to re-explain the meeting.

~>

AI tools need structured input

You've adopted Claude Code or Cursor to accelerate delivery. But AI coding assistants need structured context — file paths, implementation steps, affected modules. Meeting summaries don't provide that. You're still doing manual translation.

The meeting-to-task gap, quantified

How much engineering time leaks between discussion and implementation.

hours/week per team
on meeting follow-up
40%
of tickets missing
file or module context

to structured tasks
with contextprompt

What contextprompt gives tech leads

Repo-aware output built for the people who actually implement the work.

Real file paths, not vague summaries

When someone says "update the auth middleware," contextprompt maps that to src/middleware/auth.ts. Every extracted task includes the actual file path from your repo so developers can act immediately without guessing.

Implementation steps, not action items

Each task includes concrete implementation steps derived from the meeting discussion and your codebase structure. Output is formatted for AI coding assistants so you can paste directly into Claude Code or Cursor and start shipping.

Confidence levels per task

Each extracted task comes with a confidence level so you know which mappings are solid and which need a quick review. Low-confidence tasks flag ambiguous discussions so you can clarify before a developer goes down the wrong path.

What the output looks like

Structured tasks your team — and your AI tools — can act on immediately.

// contextprompt output — sprint planning 2026-04-21
TASK 1: Refactor JWT validation to use shared middleware
FILE: src/middleware/auth.ts
CONFIDENCE: high · EFFORT: ~2h
STEPS:
  1. Extract validateToken() from routes/auth.ts into middleware/auth.ts
  2. Add expiry check using config.JWT_EXPIRY constant
  3. Update route handlers in routes/bots.ts and routes/meetings.ts to import shared middleware
TASK 2: Fix race condition in recording state machine
FILE: src/server/recording-state.ts
CONFIDENCE: medium · EFFORT: ~3h
STEPS:
  1. Add mutex around state transitions in startRecording() and stopRecording()
  2. Reproduce with concurrent SIGUSR2 signals as described in meeting
  3. Add test case in src/server/__tests__/recording-state.test.ts

How it works for tech leads

Four steps from meeting URL to implementation-ready tasks.

1

Paste the meeting URL

Before or during your sprint planning, code review, or design meeting, paste the Zoom, Google Meet, Slack, or Teams URL into the contextprompt dashboard. The bot joins and records silently.

2

Connect your repos

Link your GitHub repos or upload a repo map from your local machine. contextprompt scans your file tree and extracts exported functions, modules, and types to build a code-aware index.

3

Tasks are extracted automatically

After the meeting ends, contextprompt matches transcript discussions to your repo structure and extracts structured tasks — each with a file path, implementation steps, confidence level, and effort estimate.

4

Assign or ship to AI tools

Push tasks directly to GitHub Issues (Pro plan), or copy the structured output into Claude Code, Cursor, or any AI coding assistant. Developers have everything they need without a single clarifying question.

★★★★★
"I used to spend 45 minutes after every sprint planning writing tickets from my notes. Now I paste the URL, review the extracted tasks, and push them to GitHub in under 10 minutes. The file path accuracy is what sold me — it just knows where things live."
Jordan K. — Tech Lead, Series B startup

Built for every meeting type tech leads run

contextprompt extracts tasks from any technical discussion — not just planning meetings.

Turn architecture discussions into implementation tasks mapped to specific modules.

Async-friendly summaries with code context so blockers get resolved faster.

Frequently asked questions

What tech leads ask before switching to contextprompt.

How does contextprompt know which files to map tasks to?

contextprompt uses a multi-stage extraction pipeline. It scans your connected repos to build a .gitignore-aware index of exported functions, classes, and modules. When the meeting transcript mentions a feature or system component, it uses bigram and keyword matching to find relevant files, then passes the transcript and source context to Claude for task extraction with real file path references.

Can I use contextprompt for code review meetings?

Yes. contextprompt works for any technical meeting where decisions are made. For code review meetings it extracts follow-up tasks (refactors, fixes, test additions) with the specific files discussed. The output is structured for immediate use in GitHub Issues or AI coding assistants.

Does the bot record the full meeting or just take notes?

The Recall.ai-powered bot joins as a participant, records audio, and generates a full transcript. It does not record video by default. The transcript is processed server-side — contextprompt does not store the raw audio after transcription. All participants see the bot in the meeting and can remove it at any time.

How does the GitHub Issues integration work?

On the Pro plan, after tasks are extracted you can push them directly to your connected GitHub repo as Issues. Each issue is pre-filled with the task title, implementation steps, affected file paths, and confidence level. You can review and edit before pushing. The integration uses the GitHub API with a token you provide during repo setup.

What AI coding assistants work with contextprompt output?

The structured output is designed to work with any AI coding assistant that accepts context. It works especially well with Claude Code (the CLI), Cursor, and GitHub Copilot Chat. You copy the task output — including file paths and implementation steps — and paste it directly as a prompt. No special integration required.

Your next planning meeting should write the tickets.
